origin.capability_match: Receive $BERA token allocation subject to vesting

attack_surface.data_description: The smart contract function or mechanism responsible for allowing Initial Core Contributors to claim their vested $BERA tokens.

security_gap.gap_description: A vulnerability in the vesting contract (e.g., reentrancy, gas griefing) or an administrative action (e.g., contract pause without clear unpause) could prevent contributors from claiming their tokens.

actor_risk.description: Initial Core Contributors (Actor ID 10) are prevented from accessing their vested $BERA tokens.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Prevents core contributors from receiving their rightful compensation, potentially damaging trust and project stability.

assumptions: Vested $BERA tokens are claimed by Initial Core Contributors through interaction with a smart contract.
The vesting smart contract could contain bugs or be subject to administrative controls that can be misused or exploited.
External factors like extreme network congestion leading to prohibitive gas costs could also temporarily deny access to claims.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct thorough security audits of the vesting contract, focusing on DoS vectors. Implement gas-efficient claim functions. Ensure any pausable functionality has clear, fair unpause conditions and is controlled by a secure, time-locked, or multi-signature mechanism. Provide alternative claim methods if feasible for extreme network conditions.

description: An Initial Core Contributor is prevented from claiming their vested $BERA tokens due to a flaw in the vesting mechanism, malicious action, or system unavailability.

trace_source.matched_capability: Receive $BERA token allocation subject to vesting
